itineraries.name: Barolo villages two-day weekend
itineraries.theme: First-time Barolo: La Morra, Barolo, Castiglione Falletto and Serralunga

days.morning: Start in La Morra with a 10:00 tasting at Trediberri in Borgata Torriglione, focused on organic La Morra Barolo from Berri and Rocche dell'Annunziata.
days.lunch: Lunch in La Morra village with a panorama over the Annunziata bowl and the Cannubi ridge beyond.
days.afternoon: Drive to Barolo for a 15:30 visit at Marchesi di Barolo, the estate at the heart of dry Barolo's 19th-century invention, then the village wine museum.
days.evening: Aperitivo and dinner in Barolo village; taste a flight across La Morra and Barolo communes.
days.accommodation: Boutique stay in La Morra or Barolo.
days.activities: Two contrasting La Morra and Barolo cellars plus the Barolo wine museum, a gentle first day pairing an organic grower with the historic house that helped invent dry Barolo.

days.morning: Drive east to Castiglione Falletto for a 10:00 visit at Cavallotto Tenuta Bricco Boschis, tasting late-released traditional Barolo from their single organic block.
days.lunch: Lunch in Castiglione Falletto or Serralunga with views of the castle ridge.
days.afternoon: Continue to Serralunga d'Alba for a 15:00 visit at Massolino, comparing Serralunga crus such as Vigna Rionda and Margheria.
days.evening: Late candlelit flight at Vinoteca Centro Storico beneath the Serralunga castle before driving back.
days.accommodation: Second night in La Morra or onward to Alba.
days.activities: Castiglione Falletto and Serralunga crus side by side, finishing with a candlelit Barolo flight under the castle to read how commune shapes the wine.

itineraries.description: Two-day introduction to Barolo across its four core villages, pairing an organic La Morra grower, the historic Marchesi di Barolo, traditional Cavallotto and Serralunga's Massolino.
itineraries.tip: Book Cavallotto and Massolino at least two weeks ahead; October weekends sell out across the Barolo zone.

itineraries.name: Barbaresco and Alba three-day loop
itineraries.theme: Barbaresco crus, the Alba truffle town and a Roero detour

days.morning: Begin in Barbaresco with a 10:00 visit at the Produttori del Barbaresco cooperative, tasting its grower single-cru bottlings.
days.lunch: Lunch in Barbaresco village beneath the medieval tower with views over the Tanaro.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Gaja for the estate that reinvented Barbaresco from 1961, then a stop at Cascina delle Rose on the Tre Stelle cru.
days.evening: Return to Alba for dinner and a late Barolo flight at Vincafe on the main street.
days.activities: Barbaresco from three angles, the grower cooperative, the house that modernised it and a low-intervention Tre Stelle estate, ending with a late flight in Alba.

days.morning: Spend the morning in Alba, browsing the truffle market and the Enoteca Fracchia & Berchialla for a cru Barolo tasting.
days.lunch: Lunch in Alba with tajarin and, in season, shaved white truffle.
days.afternoon: Drive south for a 15:30 visit at Vietti in Castiglione Falletto, comparing Barbera, Nebbiolo and single-cru Barolo.
days.evening: Dinner back in Alba; optional late tasting at Vincafe.
days.activities: An Alba day built around the truffle town and its enoteche, then a Castiglione Falletto cellar to taste Vietti's full Piedmontese range.

days.morning: Cross the Tanaro into the Roero for a 10:00 visit at Matteo Correggia in Canale, tasting Roero Arneis whites and Roero Nebbiolo reds.
days.lunch: Lunch in Canale, the unofficial capital of the Roero.
days.afternoon: Visit the Enoteca Regionale del Roero, then a relaxed Arneis tasting before the drive home.
days.evening: Optional farewell dinner in Alba or onward travel.
days.activities: A Roero finale across the river, contrasting the sandy-soil Arneis whites and lighter Nebbiolo reds with the Langhe wines of the first two days.

itineraries.description: Three days linking Barbaresco's great crus, the Alba truffle town and a Roero Arneis detour, with the Produttori cooperative, Gaja, Vietti and Matteo Correggia.
itineraries.tip: Gaja visits are limited and must be arranged well in advance through the trade; have a Produttori or Vietti booking as your anchor.

itineraries.name: Turin vermouth and Langhe four-day
itineraries.theme: From Turin aperitivo culture to the Barolo hills and Alta Langa sparkling

days.morning: Arrive in Turin and walk Piazza Castello, stopping at Caffe Mulassano for the house vermouth bianco at the marble counter.
days.lunch: Lunch in the Quadrilatero Romano near Piazza della Consolata.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Caffe Al Bicerin and the historic cafe trail, learning the 1786 Carpano vermouth story.
days.evening: Evening vermouth flight at Affini in San Salvario, the city's deepest vermouth list.
days.activities: A Turin aperitivo day tracing vermouth from its 1786 birthplace through the historic cafes to a modern San Salvario vermouth bar, framing the Langhe wines to come.

days.morning: Day trip east to Pessione for a 11:00 vermouth tasting at Casa Martini, the Martini & Rossi cellars and aperitivo museum.
days.lunch: Lunch near Chieri before driving south to the Langhe.
days.afternoon: Drive to Serralunga d'Alba and visit Fontanafredda, the former royal Savoy estate, for a Barolo tasting.
days.evening: Dinner in Serralunga or Alba; settle into the Langhe base.
days.accommodation: Move to a stay in or near Alba.
days.activities: Bridging Turin and the Langhe with the Martini vermouth cellars and the royal Fontanafredda estate, linking the city's aperitivo to Barolo's Savoy history.

days.morning: Morning at Ceretto near Alba for a Barolo and Barbaresco overview, then the Bricco Rocche cellar.
days.lunch: Lunch in Alba with a cru Barolo flight at Enoteca Fracchia & Berchialla.
days.afternoon: Afternoon visit at G.D. Vajra in Vergne, the certified-organic estate at Barolo's highest hamlet.
days.evening: Dinner in Alba and a late Barolo flight at Vincafe.
days.activities: A full Langhe day pairing Ceretto's broad range with the organic Vajra estate at Vergne, bracketed by Alba enoteca tastings.

days.morning: Drive to Canale in the Roero for a 10:30 visit at Enrico Serafino's underground cellars to taste Alta Langa traditional-method sparkling.
days.lunch: Lunch in Canale before the journey home.
days.afternoon: Optional stop at the Enoteca Regionale del Roero for a last Arneis, then depart.
days.activities: A sparkling finale in the Roero, tasting Alta Langa in the historic Enrico Serafino cellars as a contrast to the still reds of the trip.

itineraries.description: Four days from Turin's vermouth and cafe culture through the Barolo hills to Alta Langa sparkling, taking in Casa Martini, Fontanafredda, Ceretto, Vajra and Enrico Serafino.
itineraries.tip: Casa Martini is weekends-only without a booking; reserve a weekday slot if your Langhe transfer falls midweek.

itineraries.name: Traditional Barolo deep-dive three-day
itineraries.theme: Long-maceration, large-cask Barolo across Verduno, Barolo and Monforte

days.morning: Begin in Verduno with a 10:00 visit at Comm. G.B. Burlotto, tasting foot-trodden Barolo Monvigliero and the rare Verduno Pelaverga.
days.lunch: Lunch in Verduno overlooking the Monvigliero amphitheatre.
days.afternoon: Drive to Barolo village for an afternoon with Bartolo Mascarello, the traditionalist house of blended Barolo.
days.evening: Dinner in Barolo village; compare a Verduno and a Barolo-commune bottle.
days.activities: Two icons of the traditional school on day one, Burlotto's foot-trodden Monvigliero and Bartolo Mascarello's resolutely blended Barolo.

days.morning: Visit Brovia in Castiglione Falletto for four single crus farmed organically and aged in large botti.
days.lunch: Lunch in Castiglione Falletto beneath the castle.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Giacomo Conterno in Monforte, the benchmark of long-aged traditional Barolo from Cascina Francia.
days.evening: Dinner in Monforte d'Alba's old town.
days.accommodation: Second night in Barolo or Monforte.
days.activities: Castiglione Falletto and Monforte crus from two traditional houses, ending on Giacomo Conterno's long-aged Cascina Francia benchmark.

days.morning: Morning at Ettore Germano in Serralunga for high-site traditional Barolo and a sparkling Metodo Classico surprise.
days.afternoon: Finish with a candlelit flight at Vinoteca Centro Storico under the Serralunga castle before departing.
days.activities: A Serralunga close pairing Ettore Germano's high-site Barolo with a candlelit village flight, rounding off the traditional-style tour.

itineraries.description: Three days through the traditional Barolo school across Verduno, Barolo, Castiglione Falletto, Monforte and Serralunga, from Burlotto and Bartolo Mascarello to Giacomo Conterno.
itineraries.tip: Bartolo Mascarello and Giacomo Conterno are tiny and book out far ahead; secure these two first and build the rest of the route around them.
